issues.title: [DEPLOY] Isolate Health Assistant data, API, secrets, and observability
issues.body:
## Outcome

Deploy locally with stricter isolation, private networking, authentication, retention, backup, and audit controls.

## Scope

- No public Ollama or health API.
- Separate storage and backup boundary.
- Secret rotation, rate limits, request limits, session isolation, health checks, trace correlation, and content-free logs.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Health data is not mounted into Mama or Quân Sư.
- [ ] Backup/restore and deletion are verified.
- [ ] Operational metrics expose no raw health content.
- [ ] Failure does not disable unrelated ecosystem capabilities.

issues.title: [RELEASE] Execute the Health Assistant safety and Discord release gate
issues.body:
## Outcome

Enable health capability only after all safety, evidence, privacy, QA, deployment, backup, and rollback evidence passes.

## Scope

- Exact repository SHA, source registry, rule set, prompt, model, schema, and contract versions.
- Synthetic end-to-end ordinary, urgent, emergency, unavailable, malformed, and oversized cases.
- Private Discord validation, restart, upgrade, rollback, export, deletion, and backup/restore.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The release can leave health disabled without blocking generic/language/game release.
- [ ] No public response or generic fallback occurs.
- [ ] Rollback restores the last approved safety baseline.
- [ ] All evidence is secret-safe.

issues.title: [MAINTENANCE] Define source, rule, prompt, model, hazard, and incident review cadence
issues.body:
## Outcome

Maintain health behavior as a controlled safety system rather than an ordinary chatbot feature.

## Scope

- Source expiry and conflict review, rule review, model/prompt evaluation, hazard updates, incident response, security patches, contract deprecation, and emergency disable switch.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Every material change reruns affected safety tests.
- [ ] Stale sources or failed evaluations disable affected capabilities.
- [ ] Incidents preserve privacy and trigger rollback when needed.
- [ ] Scope cannot widen through maintenance changes without intended-use approval.
